1. **Duplicated properties across all 5 projects**: TargetFramework, Nullable, ImplicitUsings, TreatWarningsAsErrors, EnforceCodeStyleInBuild, Company, Authors are repeated in every project.

2. **No Directory.Build.props**: These shared properties should be centralized.

3. **Package version drift**: `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` is `8.0.0` in Core and Api but `8.0.1` in Services. 

4. **Test packages are inconsistent**: xunit, Test.Sdk, and xunit.runner.visualstudio have different versions between the two test projects.

5. **No Central Package Management**: Package versions are scattered across projects instead of being managed in one place.

6. **Duplicated custom target and properties**: The `PrintBuildInfo` target and `DocumentationFile` property are copy-pasted across all 3 src projects, and test infrastructure packages are repeated unnecessarily.

data.content:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile the full review.

---

# MSBuild Project File Review — ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Overview

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Projects** | 5 (3 src + 2 test), all SDK-style ✅ |
| **Target Framework** | `net8.0` (all projects) |
| **Directory.Build.props** | ❌ Missing |
| **Directory.Build.targets** | ❌ Missing |
| **Directory.Packages.props** | ❌ Missing (no Central Package Management) |

---

## 🔴 Errors (Likely Causing Issues)

### E1 — Package Version Drift: `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

Three projects reference the same package at **two different versions**. This means the actual version resolved at build time depends on NuGet's conflict resolution algorithm, which can produce silent, confusing runtime behavior.

| Project | Version |
|---|---|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |

`ContosoApp.Api` references both `Core` (8.0.0) and `Services` (8.0.1), so NuGet will silently unify to 8.0.1 — but this is implicit and fragile.

**Fix:** Centralize all versions via Central Package Management (see suggestion S1 below).

---

### E2 — Test Framework Version Drift Across Test Projects

The two test projects use **different versions** of the same test infrastructure:

| Package | `Core.Tests` | `Api.Tests` |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

This can lead to inconsistent test behavior between the two projects. Both should use the same test infrastructure versions.

**Fix:** Centralize via CPM (see S1), or at minimum align the versions manually.

---

## 🟡 Warnings (Anti-Patterns)

### W1 — Massive Property Duplication Across All 5 Projects

Every single `.csproj` file repeats this identical block:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

That's **7 properties × 5 projects = 35 lines** of pure duplication. When you need to update `TargetFramework` to `net9.0`, you'd need to touch all 5 files.

**Fix:** Introduce a `Directory.Build.props` at the repo root (see S2 below).

---

### W2 — Duplicated `DocumentationFile` Property in 3 Source Projects

All three `src/` projects contain an identical second `<PropertyGroup>`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

This should be centralized. It's also only appropriate for source (packable/publishable) projects, not test projects — so it belongs in either `Directory.Build.props` with a condition, or in a shared `.props` file imported only by `src/` projects.

**Fix:** Move into `Directory.Build.props` with a condition to exclude test projects:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsPackable)' != 'false'">
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

> ⚠️ **Note:** This uses a condition on `$(IsPackable)` which is a property, not `$(TargetFramework)`. Since `IsPackable` is set explicitly in the project files (not inferred from SDK evaluation), this condition is safe in `.props`. However, if you later change the detection logic to rely on `$(TargetFramework)` or other late-evaluated properties, move it to `Directory.Build.targets`.

---

### W3 — Duplicated Custom Target `PrintBuildInfo` in 3 Source Projects

An identical target exists in `Core`, `Services`, and `Api`:

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

Duplicated targets are a maintenance burden and can easily drift. This belongs in `Directory.Build.targets`.

**Fix:** Create `Directory.Build.targets` at the repo root:

```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

Then remove the target from all three `.csproj` files.

---

### W4 — Unnecessary Explicit `System.Text.Json` in the Web Project

`ContosoApp.Api` uses `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web`, which already includes `System.Text.Json` as part of the ASP.NET Core shared framework for `net8.0`. The explicit reference:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
```

is unnecessary in the web project and can cause subtle version conflicts if the pinned version diverges from the framework's bundled version.

**Fix:** Remove the explicit `System.Text.Json` reference from `ContosoApp.Api.csproj`. If the library projects (`Core`) need a specific version, those references are correct since `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` doesn't include it implicitly.

---

### W5 — Scattered `IsPackable=false` Across Test Projects

Both test projects set `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>` individually. This is a common pattern that benefits from centralization — especially as the number of test projects grows.

**Fix:** In `Directory.Build.props`, use a condition based on project path:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectFullPath.Contains('test'))">
  <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
</PropertyGroup>
```

---

### W6 — Split `PropertyGroup` Blocks in Source Projects

`Core`, `Services`, and `Api` each have **two separate `<PropertyGroup>` blocks** with no differentiating condition. For example in `Core`:

```xml
<!-- Lines 3-11: first PropertyGroup -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  ...
</PropertyGroup>

<!-- Lines 18-20: second PropertyGroup, no condition -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>...</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Multiple `<PropertyGroup>` blocks are only warranted when they have different `Condition` attributes. Otherwise, consolidate for readability.

---

## 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements)

### S1 — Introduce NuGet Central Package Management (CPM)

With 5 projects and 8 distinct packages, versions are already drifting (see E1, E2). CPM eliminates this class of bug entirely.

**Create `/Directory.Packages.props`:**

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />

    <!-- Testing -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**Then in each `.csproj`, remove all `Version=` attributes:**

```xml
<!-- BEFORE -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />

<!-- AFTER -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit" />
```

---

### S2 — Introduce `Directory.Build.props`

**Create `/Directory.Build.props`:**

```xml
<Project>

  <!-- Shared across all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```
